meta_title: For Founders: analytics setup to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Analytics Setup to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop guessing what to build. Learn how to implement analytics-driven validation to solve the 'no project ideas' trap using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Stop chasing abstract ideas. The cure for 'no project ideas' is installing lightweight analytics on a landing page prototype. By tracking visitor intent and 'intent-to-buy' clicks, you let market data dictate your product roadmap, turning cold traffic into high-conviction building requirements instead of guessing in the dark.
content.intro:
The blank screen is a founder's graveyard. You have the technical skills, the stack, and the ambition, but you are paralyzed by the 'no project ideas' loop, spinning wheels on features nobody requested. The silent failure of most SaaS startups isn't bad code; it's the lack of a feedback loop.

True innovation doesn't happen in a vacuum—it happens at the intersection of user behavior and data. By deploying a lean analytics setup today, you shift from 'guessing' to 'observing.' We are going to use Antigravity and Claude Code to bootstrap an analytics-first landing page that forces your market to tell you exactly what they are willing to pay for.

steps.title: Define Your Conversion North Star
steps.description: Identify the one metric that proves market interest, such as an email signup or a 'Join Waitlist' click.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a schema for a landing page analytics event that tracks 'intent_to_buy' clicks with metadata on user device and referral source.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --dependency posthog-js --description 'Initialize PostHog analytics for conversion tracking'

steps.title: Bootstrap the Analytics Layer
steps.description: Inject lightweight tracking code into your MVP structure to ensure you capture every visitor interaction without bloating load times.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, modular component that initializes analytics tracking and wraps high-value CTA buttons with event listeners.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create analytics wrapper component and inject into index.tsx'

steps.title: Configure Event Funnels
steps.description: Set up the backend to filter noise and focus strictly on conversion-related behavior so you can validate ideas quickly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a configuration script that filters analytics events to trigger only when a user engages with the 'pricing' or 'sign-up' call-to-actions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit src/analytics.ts --prompt 'configure event filtering to isolate high-intent user journeys'

steps.title: Automate Insight Reporting
steps.description: Create a dashboard view that summarizes visitor intent, allowing you to iterate on your idea based on actual data.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a dashboard summary view that aggregates 'intent_to_buy' events by referral source to identify high-potential niches.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate a dashboard component to display conversion metrics from current analytics data'

steps.title: Iterate Based on Real Signals
steps.description: Use the data to pivot your landing page messaging, effectively testing multiple 'project ideas' without writing a single line of backend product code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a split-test framework for the landing page copy based on the top-performing analytics event categories.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --feature 'A/B testing framework for copy variations'

pro_tips: Never track everything. If you track every click, you’ll drown in noise. Track only 'intent' events that represent a user moving closer to a wallet-out moment.
Use UTM parameters in your links before you even build the site to see which problem-space has the highest click-through rate.
If your analytics show zero clicks after 100 visitors, your problem is the value proposition, not the features. Pivot the copy before writing more code.
Integrate a 'feedback' button that triggers only after a user stays on the page for 30 seconds to capture qualitative 'why' data.
Keep your analytics stack under 50kb. Bloated tracking scripts kill page load speed, which is a leading cause of bounce rates for new SaaS ideas.
content.next_steps: Once your analytics are live, drive 100 targeted visitors to your page via social or cold outreach. Analyze the 'intent' clicks, identify the winning value proposition, and begin building the MVP for that specific problem.
content.conversion_block: Stop building for yourself and start building for the market. By validating through data, you eliminate the guesswork that kills most SaaS dreams before they launch. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
